1→package cli
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"errors"
     6→	"fmt"
     7→	"io"
     8→	"os"
     9→	"path/filepath"
    10→	"strings"
    11→
    12→	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent"
    13→	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/paths"
    14→	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/session"
    15→	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/strategy"
    16→
    17→	"github.com/charmbracelet/huh"
    18→	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
    19→	"github.com/spf13/pflag"
    20→)
    21→
    22→// Strategy display names for user-friendly selection
    23→const (
    24→	strategyDisplayManualCommit = "manual-commit"
    25→	strategyDisplayAutoCommit   = "auto-commit"
    26→)
    27→
    28→// Config path display strings
    29→const (
    30→	configDisplayProject = ".entire/settings.json"
    31→	configDisplayLocal   = ".entire/settings.local.json"
    32→)
    33→
    34→// strategyDisplayToInternal maps user-friendly names to internal strategy names
    35→var strategyDisplayToInternal = map[string]string{
    36→	strategyDisplayManualCommit: strategy.StrategyNameManualCommit,
    37→	strategyDisplayAutoCommit:   strategy.StrategyNameAutoCommit,
    38→}
    39→
    40→// strategyInternalToDisplay maps internal strategy names to user-friendly names
    41→var strategyInternalToDisplay = map[string]string{
    42→	strategy.StrategyNameManualCommit: strategyDisplayManualCommit,
    43→	strategy.StrategyNameAutoCommit:   strategyDisplayAutoCommit,
    44→}
    45→
    46→func newEnableCmd() *cobra.Command {
    47→	var localDev bool
    48→	var ignoreUntracked bool
    49→	var useLocalSettings bool
    50→	var useProjectSettings bool
    51→	var agentName string
    52→	var strategyFlag string
    53→	var forceHooks bool
    54→	var skipPushSessions bool
    55→	var telemetry bool
    56→
    57→	cmd := &cobra.Command{
    58→		Use:   "enable",
    59→		Short: "Enable Entire in current project",
    60→		Long: `Enable Entire with session tracking for your AI agent workflows.
    61→
    62→Uses the manual-commit strategy by default. To use a different strategy:
    63→
    64→  entire enable --strategy auto-commit
    65→
    66→Strategies: manual-commit (default), auto-commit`,
    67→		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, _ []string) error {
    68→			// Check if we're in a git repository first - this is a prerequisite error,
    69→			// not a usage error, so we silence Cobra's output and use SilentError
    70→			// to prevent duplicate error output in main.go
    71→			if _, err := paths.RepoRoot(); err != nil {
    72→				fmt.Fprintln(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "Not a git repository. Please run 'entire enable' from within a git repository.")
    73→				return NewSilentError(errors.New("not a git repository"))
    74→			}
    75→
    76→			if err := validateSetupFlags(useLocalSettings, useProjectSettings); err != nil {
    77→				return err
    78→			}
    79→
    80→			// Warn if repo has no commits yet
    81→			if repo, err := strategy.OpenRepository(); err == nil && strategy.IsEmptyRepository(repo) {
    82→				fmt.Fprintln(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "Note: This repository has no commits yet. Entire will be configured, but")
    83→				fmt.Fprintln(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "session checkpoints won't work until you create your first commit.")
    84→				fmt.Fprintln(cmd.OutOrStdout())
    85→			}
    86→
    87→			// Non-interactive mode if --agent flag is provided
    88→			if cmd.Flags().Changed("agent") && agentName == "" {
    89→				printMissingAgentError(cmd.ErrOrStderr())
    90→				return NewSilentError(errors.New("missing agent name"))
    91→			}
    92→
    93→			if agentName != "" {
    94→				ag, err := agent.Get(agent.AgentName(agentName))
    95→				if err != nil {
    96→					printWrongAgentError(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), agentName)
    97→					return NewSilentError(errors.New("wrong agent name"))
    98→				}
    99→				return setupAgentHooksNonInteractive(cmd.OutOrStdout(), ag, strategyFlag, localDev, forceHooks, skipPushSessions, telemetry)
   100→			}
   101→			// If strategy is specified via flag, skip interactive selection
   102→			if strategyFlag != "" {
   103→				return runEnableWithStrategy(cmd.OutOrStdout(), strategyFlag, localDev, ignoreUntracked, useLocalSettings, useProjectSettings, forceHooks, skipPushSessions, telemetry)
   104→			}
   105→			return runEnableInteractive(cmd.OutOrStdout(), localDev, ignoreUntracked, useLocalSettings, useProjectSettings, forceHooks, skipPushSessions, telemetry)
   106→		},
   107→	}
   108→
   109→	cmd.Flags().BoolVar(&localDev, "local-dev", false, "Use go run instead of entire binary for hooks")
   110→	cmd.Flags().MarkHidden("local-dev") //nolint:errcheck,gosec // flag is defined above
   111→	cmd.Flags().BoolVar(&ignoreUntracked, "ignore-untracked", false, "Commit all new files without tracking pre-existing untracked files")
   112→	cmd.Flags().MarkHidden("ignore-untracked") //nolint:errcheck,gosec // flag is defined above
   113→	cmd.Flags().BoolVar(&useLocalSettings, "local", false, "Write settings to .entire/settings.local.json instead of .entire/settings.json")
   114→	cmd.Flags().BoolVar(&useProjectSettings, "project", false, "Write settings to .entire/settings.json even if it already exists")
   115→	cmd.Flags().StringVar(&agentName, "agent", "", "Agent to setup hooks for (e.g., claude-code). Enables non-interactive mode.")
   116→	cmd.Flags().StringVar(&strategyFlag, "strategy", "", "Strategy to use (manual-commit or auto-commit)")
   117→	cmd.Flags().BoolVarP(&forceHooks, "force", "f", false, "Force reinstall hooks (removes existing Entire hooks first)")
   118→	cmd.Flags().BoolVar(&skipPushSessions, "skip-push-sessions", false, "Disable automatic pushing of session logs on git push")
   119→	cmd.Flags().BoolVar(&telemetry, "telemetry", true, "Enable anonymous usage analytics")
   120→	//nolint:errcheck,gosec // completion is optional, flag is defined above
   121→	cmd.RegisterFlagCompletionFunc("strategy", func(_ *cobra.Command, _ []string, _ string) ([]string, cobra.ShellCompDirective) {
   122→		return []string{strategyDisplayManualCommit, strategyDisplayAutoCommit}, cobra.ShellCompDirectiveNoFileComp
   123→	})
   124→
   125→	// Provide a helpful error when --agent is used without a value
   126→	defaultFlagErr := cmd.FlagErrorFunc()
   127→	cmd.SetFlagErrorFunc(func(c *cobra.Command, err error) error {
   128→		var valErr *pflag.ValueRequiredError
   129→		if errors.As(err, &valErr) && valErr.GetSpecifiedName() == "agent" {
   130→			printMissingAgentError(c.ErrOrStderr())
   131→			return NewSilentError(errors.New("missing agent name"))
   132→		}
   133→		return defaultFlagErr(c, err)
   134→	})
   135→
   136→	// Add subcommands for automation/testing
   137→	cmd.AddCommand(newSetupGitHookCmd())
   138→
   139→	return cmd
   140→}
   141→
   142→func newDisableCmd() *cobra.Command {
   143→	var useProjectSettings bool
   144→	var uninstall bool
   145→	var force bool
   146→
   147→	cmd := &cobra.Command{
   148→		Use:   "disable",
   149→		Short: "Disable Entire in current project",
   150→		Long: `Disable Entire integrations in the current project.
   151→
   152→By default, this command will disable Entire. Hooks will exit silently and commands will
   153→show a disabled message.
   154→
   155→To completely remove Entire integrations from this repository, use --uninstall:
   156→  - .entire/ directory (settings, logs, metadata)
   157→  - Git hooks (prepare-commit-msg, commit-msg, post-commit, pre-push)
   158→  - Session state files (.git/entire-sessions/)
   159→  - Shadow branches (entire/<hash>)
   160→  - Agent hooks (Claude Code, Gemini CLI)`,
   161→		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, _ []string) error {
   162→			if uninstall {
   163→				return runUninstall(cmd.OutOrStdout(), cmd.ErrOrStderr(), force)
   164→			}
   165→			return runDisable(cmd.OutOrStdout(), useProjectSettings)
   166→		},
   167→	}
   168→
   169→	cmd.Flags().BoolVar(&useProjectSettings, "project", false, "Update .entire/settings.json instead of .entire/settings.local.json")
   170→	cmd.Flags().BoolVar(&uninstall, "uninstall", false, "Completely remove Entire from this repository")
   171→	cmd.Flags().BoolVar(&force, "force", false, "Skip confirmation prompt (use with --uninstall)")
   172→
   173→	return cmd
   174→}
   175→
   176→// isFullyEnabled checks whether Entire is already fully set up.
   177→// Returns whether it's fully enabled, and if so, the agent type display name and config file path.
   178→func isFullyEnabled() (enabled bool, agentDesc string, configPath string) {
   179→	// Check settings exist and Enabled == true
   180→	s, err := LoadEntireSettings()
   181→	if err != nil || !s.Enabled {
   182→		return false, "", ""
   183→	}
   184→
   185→	// Check any agent hooks installed (not just Claude Code — works with Gemini too)
   186→	installedAgents := GetAgentsWithHooksInstalled()
   187→	if len(installedAgents) == 0 {
   188→		return false, "", ""
   189→	}
   190→
   191→	// Check git hooks installed
   192→	if !strategy.IsGitHookInstalled() {
   193→		return false, "", ""
   194→	}
   195→
   196→	// Check .entire directory exists
   197→	if !checkEntireDirExists() {
   198→		return false, "", ""
   199→	}
   200→
   201→	// Determine agent description from first installed agent
   202→	desc := string(installedAgents[0]) // fallback to agent name
   203→	if ag, err := agent.Get(installedAgents[0]); err == nil {
   204→		desc = string(ag.Type())
   205→	}
   206→
   207→	// Determine config path - check if local settings exists, otherwise show project settings
   208→	entireDirAbs, err := paths.AbsPath(paths.EntireDir)
   209→	if err != nil {
   210→		entireDirAbs = paths.EntireDir
   211→	}
   212→	configDisplay := configDisplayProject
   213→	localSettingsPath := filepath.Join(entireDirAbs, "settings.local.json")
   214→	if _, err := os.Stat(localSettingsPath); err == nil {
   215→		configDisplay = configDisplayLocal
   216→	}
   217→
   218→	return true, desc, configDisplay
   219→}
   220→
   221→// runEnableWithStrategy enables Entire with a specified strategy (non-interactive).
   222→// The selectedStrategy can be either a display name (manual-commit, auto-commit)
   223→// or an internal name (manual-commit, auto-commit).
   224→func runEnableWithStrategy(w io.Writer, selectedStrategy string, localDev, _, useLocalSettings, useProjectSettings, forceHooks, skipPushSessions, telemetry bool) error {
   225→	// Map the strategy to internal name if it's a display name
   226→	internalStrategy := selectedStrategy
   227→	if mapped, ok := strategyDisplayToInternal[selectedStrategy]; ok {
   228→		internalStrategy = mapped
   229→	}
   230→
   231→	// Validate the strategy exists
   232→	strat, err := strategy.Get(internalStrategy)
   233→	if err != nil {
   234→		return fmt.Errorf("unknown strategy: %s (use manual-commit or auto-commit)", selectedStrategy)
   235→	}
   236→
   237→	// Detect default agent
   238→	ag := agent.Default()
   239→	agentType := string(agent.AgentTypeClaudeCode)
   240→	if ag != nil {
   241→		agentType = string(ag.Type())
   242→	}
   243→	fmt.Fprintf(w, "Agent: %s (use --agent to change)\n\n", agentType)
   244→
   245→	// Setup Claude Code hooks (agent hooks don't depend on settings)
   246→	if _, err := setupClaudeCodeHook(localDev, forceHooks); err != nil {
   247→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to setup Claude Code hooks: %w", err)
   248→	}
   249→
   250→	// Setup .entire directory
   251→	if _, err := setupEntireDirectory(); err != nil {
   252→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to setup .entire directory: %w", err)
   253→	}
   254→
   255→	// Load existing settings to preserve other options (like strategy_options.push)
   256→	settings, err := LoadEntireSettings()
   257→	if err != nil {
   258→		// If we can't load, start with defaults
   259→		settings = &EntireSettings{}
   260→	}
   261→	// Update the specific fields
   262→	settings.Strategy = internalStrategy
   263→	settings.LocalDev = localDev
   264→	settings.Enabled = true
   265→
   266→	// Set push_sessions option if --skip-push-sessions flag was provided
   267→	if skipPushSessions {
   268→		if settings.StrategyOptions == nil {
   269→			settings.StrategyOptions = make(map[string]interface{})
   270→		}
   271→		settings.StrategyOptions["push_sessions"] = false
   272→	}
   273→
   274→	// Handle telemetry for non-interactive mode
   275→	// Note: if telemetry is nil (not configured), it defaults to disabled
   276→	if !telemetry || os.Getenv("ENTIRE_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT") != "" {
   277→		f := false
   278→		settings.Telemetry = &f
   279→	}
   280→
   281→	// Determine which settings file to write to
   282→	entireDirAbs, err := paths.AbsPath(paths.EntireDir)
   283→	if err != nil {
   284→		entireDirAbs = paths.EntireDir // Fallback to relative
   285→	}
   286→	shouldUseLocal, showNotification := determineSettingsTarget(entireDirAbs, useLocalSettings, useProjectSettings)
   287→
   288→	if showNotification {
   289→		fmt.Fprintln(w, "Info: Project settings exist. Saving to settings.local.json instead.")
   290→		fmt.Fprintln(w, "  Use --project to update the project settings file.")
   291→	}
   292→
   293→	configDisplay := configDisplayProject
   294→	if shouldUseLocal {
   295→		if err := SaveEntireSettingsLocal(settings); err != nil {
   296→			return fmt.Errorf("failed to save local settings: %w", err)
   297→		}
   298→		configDisplay = configDisplayLocal
   299→	} else {
   300→		if err := SaveEntireSettings(settings); err != nil {
   301→			return fmt.Errorf("failed to save settings: %w", err)
   302→		}
   303→	}
   304→
   305→	// Install git hooks AFTER saving settings (InstallGitHook reads local_dev from settings)
   306→	if _, err := strategy.InstallGitHook(true); err != nil {
   307→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to install git hooks: %w", err)
   308→	}
   309→	fmt.Fprintln(w, "✓ Hooks installed")
   310→	fmt.Fprintf(w, "✓ Project configured (%s)\n", configDisplay)
   311→
   312→	// Let the strategy handle its own setup requirements
   313→	if err := strat.EnsureSetup(); err != nil {
   314→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to setup strategy: %w", err)
   315→	}
   316→
   317→	fmt.Fprintln(w, "\nReady.")
   318→
   319→	return nil
   320→}
   321→
   322→// runEnableInteractive runs the interactive enable flow.
   323→func runEnableInteractive(w io.Writer, localDev, _, useLocalSettings, useProjectSettings, forceHooks, skipPushSessions, telemetry bool) error {
   324→	// Check if already fully enabled — show summary and return early.
   325→	// Skip early return if any configuration flags are set (user wants to reconfigure).
   326→	hasConfigFlags := forceHooks || skipPushSessions || !telemetry || useLocalSettings || useProjectSettings || localDev
   327→	if !hasConfigFlags {
   328→		if fullyEnabled, agentDesc, configPath := isFullyEnabled(); fullyEnabled {
   329→			fmt.Fprintln(w, "Already enabled. Everything looks good.")
   330→			fmt.Fprintln(w)
   331→			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  Agent: %s\n", agentDesc)
   332→			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  Config: %s\n", configPath)
   333→			return nil
   334→		}
   335→	}
   336→
   337→	// Detect default agent
   338→	ag := agent.Default()
   339→	agentType := string(agent.AgentTypeClaudeCode)
   340→	if ag != nil {
   341→		agentType = string(ag.Type())
   342→	}
   343→	fmt.Fprintf(w, "Agent: %s (use --agent to change)\n\n", agentType)
   344→
   345→	// Setup Claude Code hooks (agent hooks don't depend on settings)
   346→	if _, err := setupClaudeCodeHook(localDev, forceHooks); err != nil {
   347→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to setup Claude Code hooks: %w", err)
   348→	}
   349→
   350→	// Setup .entire directory
   351→	if _, err := setupEntireDirectory(); err != nil {
   352→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to setup .entire directory: %w", err)
   353→	}
   354→
   355→	// Use the default strategy (manual-commit)
   356→	internalStrategy := strategy.DefaultStrategyName
   357→
   358→	// Load existing settings to preserve other options (like strategy_options.push)
   359→	settings, err := LoadEntireSettings()
   360→	if err != nil {
   361→		// If we can't load, start with defaults
   362→		settings = &EntireSettings{}
   363→	}
   364→	// Update the specific fields
   365→	settings.Strategy = internalStrategy
   366→	settings.LocalDev = localDev
   367→	settings.Enabled = true
   368→
   369→	// Set push_sessions option if --skip-push-sessions flag was provided
   370→	if skipPushSessions {
   371→		if settings.StrategyOptions == nil {
   372→			settings.StrategyOptions = make(map[string]interface{})
   373→		}
   374→		settings.StrategyOptions["push_sessions"] = false
   375→	}
   376→
   377→	// Determine which settings file to write to
   378→	// First run always creates settings.json (no prompt)
   379→	entireDirAbs, err := paths.AbsPath(paths.EntireDir)
   380→	if err != nil {
   381→		entireDirAbs = paths.EntireDir // Fallback to relative
   382→	}
   383→	shouldUseLocal, showNotification := determineSettingsTarget(entireDirAbs, useLocalSettings, useProjectSettings)
   384→
   385→	if showNotification {
   386→		fmt.Fprintln(w, "Info: Project settings exist. Saving to settings.local.json instead.")
   387→		fmt.Fprintln(w, "  Use --project to update the project settings file.")
   388→	}
   389→
   390→	// Helper to save settings to the appropriate file
   391→	saveSettings := func() error {
   392→		if shouldUseLocal {
   393→			return SaveEntireSettingsLocal(settings)
   394→		}
   395→		return SaveEntireSettings(settings)
   396→	}
   397→
   398→	// Save settings before telemetry prompt so config is persisted even if the user cancels
   399→	if err := saveSettings(); err != nil {
   400→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to save settings: %w", err)
   401→	}
   402→
   403→	// Install git hooks AFTER saving settings (InstallGitHook reads local_dev from settings)
   404→	if _, err := strategy.InstallGitHook(true); err != nil {
   405→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to install git hooks: %w", err)
   406→	}
   407→	fmt.Fprintln(w, "✓ Hooks installed")
   408→
   409→	configDisplay := configDisplayProject
   410→	if shouldUseLocal {
   411→		configDisplay = configDisplayLocal
   412→	}
   413→	fmt.Fprintf(w, "✓ Project configured (%s)\n", configDisplay)
   414→
   415→	// Ask about telemetry consent (only if not already asked)
   416→	fmt.Fprintln(w)
   417→	if err := promptTelemetryConsent(settings, telemetry); err != nil {
   418→		return fmt.Errorf("telemetry consent: %w", err)
   419→	}
   420→	// Save again to persist telemetry choice
   421→	if err := saveSettings(); err != nil {
   422→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to save settings: %w", err)
   423→	}
   424→
   425→	// Let the strategy handle its own setup requirements
   426→	strat, err := strategy.Get(internalStrategy)
   427→	if err != nil {
   428→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get strategy: %w", err)
   429→	}
   430→	if err := strat.EnsureSetup(); err != nil {
   431→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to setup strategy: %w", err)
   432→	}
   433→
   434→	fmt.Fprintln(w, "\nReady.")
   435→
   436→	return nil
   437→}

   439→// runEnable is a simple enable that just sets the enabled flag (for programmatic use).
   440→func runEnable(w io.Writer) error {
   441→	settings, err := LoadEntireSettings()
   442→	if err != nil {
   443→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to load settings: %w", err)
   444→	}
   445→
   446→	settings.Enabled = true
   447→	if err := SaveEntireSettings(settings); err != nil {
   448→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to save settings: %w", err)
   449→	}
   450→
   451→	fmt.Fprintln(w, "Entire is now enabled.")
   452→	return nil
   453→}
   454→
   455→func runDisable(w io.Writer, useProjectSettings bool) error {
   456→	settings, err := LoadEntireSettings()
   457→	if err != nil {
   458→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to load settings: %w", err)
   459→	}
   460→
   461→	settings.Enabled = false
   462→
   463→	// If --project flag is specified, always write to project settings
   464→	if useProjectSettings {
   465→		if err := SaveEntireSettings(settings); err != nil {
   466→			return fmt.Errorf("failed to save settings: %w", err)
   467→		}
   468→	} else {
   469→		// Always write to local settings file (create if doesn't exist)
   470→		if err := SaveEntireSettingsLocal(settings); err != nil {
   471→			return fmt.Errorf("failed to save local settings: %w", err)
   472→		}
   473→	}
   474→
   475→	fmt.Fprintln(w, "Entire is now disabled.")
   476→	return nil
   477→}

   479→// DisabledMessage is the message shown when Entire is disabled
   480→const DisabledMessage = "Entire is disabled. Run `entire enable` to re-enable."
   481→
   482→// checkDisabledGuard checks if Entire is disabled and prints a message if so.
   483→// Returns true if the caller should exit (i.e., Entire is disabled).
   484→// On error reading settings, defaults to enabled (returns false).
   485→func checkDisabledGuard(w io.Writer) bool {
   486→	enabled, err := IsEnabled()
   487→	if err != nil {
   488→		// Default to enabled on error
   489→		return false
   490→	}
   491→	if !enabled {
   492→		fmt.Fprintln(w, DisabledMessage)
   493→		return true
   494→	}
   495→	return false
   496→}

   546→// setupAgentHooksNonInteractive sets up hooks for a specific agent non-interactively.
   547→// If strategyName is provided, it sets the strategy; otherwise uses default.
   548→func setupAgentHooksNonInteractive(w io.Writer, ag agent.Agent, strategyName string, localDev, forceHooks, skipPushSessions, telemetry bool) error {
   549→	agentName := ag.Name()
   550→	// Check if agent supports hooks
   551→	hookAgent, ok := ag.(agent.HookSupport)
   552→	if !ok {
   553→		return fmt.Errorf("agent %s does not support hooks", agentName)
   554→	}
   555→
   556→	fmt.Fprintf(w, "Agent: %s\n\n", ag.Type())
   557→
   558→	// Install agent hooks (agent hooks don't depend on settings)
   559→	installedHooks, err := hookAgent.InstallHooks(localDev, forceHooks)
   560→	if err != nil {
   561→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to install hooks for %s: %w", agentName, err)
   562→	}
   563→
   564→	// Setup .entire directory
   565→	if _, err := setupEntireDirectory(); err != nil {
   566→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to setup .entire directory: %w", err)
   567→	}
   568→
   569→	// Load existing settings to preserve other options (like strategy_options.push)
   570→	settings, err := LoadEntireSettings()
   571→	if err != nil {
   572→		// If we can't load, start with defaults
   573→		settings = &EntireSettings{Strategy: strategy.DefaultStrategyName}
   574→	}
   575→	settings.Enabled = true
   576→	if localDev {
   577→		settings.LocalDev = localDev
   578→	}
   579→
   580→	// Set push_sessions option if --skip-push-sessions flag was provided
   581→	if skipPushSessions {
   582→		if settings.StrategyOptions == nil {
   583→			settings.StrategyOptions = make(map[string]interface{})
   584→		}
   585→		settings.StrategyOptions["push_sessions"] = false
   586→	}
   587→
   588→	// Set strategy if provided
   589→	if strategyName != "" {
   590→		// Map display name to internal name if needed
   591→		internalStrategy := strategyName
   592→		if mapped, ok := strategyDisplayToInternal[strategyName]; ok {
   593→			internalStrategy = mapped
   594→		}
   595→		// Validate the strategy exists
   596→		if _, err := strategy.Get(internalStrategy); err != nil {
   597→			return fmt.Errorf("unknown strategy: %s (use manual-commit or auto-commit)", strategyName)
   598→		}
   599→		settings.Strategy = internalStrategy
   600→	}
   601→
   602→	// Handle telemetry for non-interactive mode
   603→	// Note: if telemetry is nil (not configured), it defaults to disabled
   604→	if !telemetry || os.Getenv("ENTIRE_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT") != "" {
   605→		f := false
   606→		settings.Telemetry = &f
   607→	}
   608→
   609→	if err := SaveEntireSettings(settings); err != nil {
   610→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to save settings: %w", err)
   611→	}
   612→
   613→	// Install git hooks AFTER saving settings (InstallGitHook reads local_dev from settings)
   614→	if _, err := strategy.InstallGitHook(true); err != nil {
   615→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to install git hooks: %w", err)
   616→	}
   617→
   618→	if installedHooks == 0 {
   619→		msg := fmt.Sprintf("Hooks for %s already installed", ag.Description())
   620→		if agentName == agent.AgentNameGemini {
   621→			msg += " (Preview)"
   622→		}
   623→		fmt.Fprintf(w, "%s\n", msg)
   624→	} else {
   625→		msg := fmt.Sprintf("Installed %d hooks for %s", installedHooks, ag.Description())
   626→		if agentName == agent.AgentNameGemini {
   627→			msg += " (Preview)"
   628→		}
   629→		fmt.Fprintf(w, "%s\n", msg)
   630→	}
   631→
   632→	fmt.Fprintf(w, "✓ Project configured (%s)\n", configDisplayProject)
   633→
   634→	// Let the strategy handle its own setup requirements (creates entire/checkpoints/v1 branch, etc.)
   635→	strat, err := strategy.Get(settings.Strategy)
   636→	if err != nil {
   637→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get strategy: %w", err)
   638→	}
   639→	if err := strat.EnsureSetup(); err != nil {
   640→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to setup strategy: %w", err)
   641→	}
   642→
   643→	fmt.Fprintln(w, "\nReady.")
   644→
   645→	return nil
   646→}

   648→// validateSetupFlags checks that --local and --project flags are not both specified.
   649→func validateSetupFlags(useLocal, useProject bool) error {
   650→	if useLocal && useProject {
   651→		return errors.New("cannot specify both --project and --local")
   652→	}
   653→	return nil
   654→}
   655→
   656→// determineSettingsTarget decides whether to write to settings.local.json based on:
   657→// - Whether settings.json already exists
   658→// - The --local and --project flags
   659→// Returns (useLocal, showNotification).
   660→func determineSettingsTarget(entireDir string, useLocal, useProject bool) (bool, bool) {
   661→	// Explicit --local flag always uses local settings
   662→	if useLocal {
   663→		return true, false
   664→	}
   665→
   666→	// Explicit --project flag always uses project settings
   667→	if useProject {
   668→		return false, false
   669→	}
   670→
   671→	// No flags specified - check if settings file exists
   672→	settingsPath := filepath.Join(entireDir, paths.SettingsFileName)
   673→	if _, err := os.Stat(settingsPath); err == nil {
   674→		// Settings file exists - auto-redirect to local with notification
   675→		return true, true
   676→	}
   677→
   678→	// Settings file doesn't exist - create it
   679→	return false, false
   680→}

   682→// setupEntireDirectory creates the .entire directory and gitignore.
   683→// Returns true if the directory was created, false if it already existed.
   684→func setupEntireDirectory() (bool, error) { //nolint:unparam // already present in codebase
   685→	// Get absolute path for the .entire directory
   686→	entireDirAbs, err := paths.AbsPath(paths.EntireDir)
   687→	if err != nil {
   688→		entireDirAbs = paths.EntireDir // Fallback to relative
   689→	}
   690→
   691→	// Check if directory already exists
   692→	created := false
   693→	if _, err := os.Stat(entireDirAbs); os.IsNotExist(err) {
   694→		created = true
   695→	}
   696→
   697→	// Create .entire directory
   698→	//nolint:gosec // G301: Project directory needs standard permissions for git
   699→	if err := os.MkdirAll(entireDirAbs, 0o755); err != nil {
   700→		return false, fmt.Errorf("failed to create .entire directory: %w", err)
   701→	}
   702→
   703→	// Create/update .gitignore with all required entries
   704→	if err := strategy.EnsureEntireGitignore(); err != nil {
   705→		return false, fmt.Errorf("failed to setup .gitignore: %w", err)
   706→	}
   707→
   708→	return created, nil
   709→}

   750→
   751→// shellCompletionComment is the comment preceding the completion line
   752→const shellCompletionComment = "# Entire CLI shell completion"
   753→
   754→// errUnsupportedShell is returned when the user's shell is not supported for completion.
   755→var errUnsupportedShell = errors.New("unsupported shell")
   756→
   757→// shellCompletionTarget returns the rc file path and completion lines for the
   758→// user's current shell.
   759→func shellCompletionTarget() (shellName, rcFile, completionLine string, err error) {
   760→	home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
   761→	if err != nil {
   762→		return "", "", "", fmt.Errorf("cannot determine home directory: %w", err)
   763→	}
   764→
   765→	shell := os.Getenv("SHELL")
   766→	switch {
   767→	case strings.Contains(shell, "zsh"):
   768→		return "Zsh",
   769→			filepath.Join(home, ".zshrc"),
   770→			"autoload -Uz compinit && compinit && source <(entire completion zsh)",
   771→			nil
   772→	case strings.Contains(shell, "bash"):
   773→		bashRC := filepath.Join(home, ".bashrc")
   774→		if _, err := os.Stat(filepath.Join(home, ".bash_profile")); err == nil {
   775→			bashRC = filepath.Join(home, ".bash_profile")
   776→		}
   777→		return "Bash",
   778→			bashRC,
   779→			"source <(entire completion bash)",
   780→			nil
   781→	case strings.Contains(shell, "fish"):
   782→		return "Fish",
   783→			filepath.Join(home, ".config", "fish", "config.fish"),
   784→			"entire completion fish | source",
   785→			nil
   786→	default:
   787→		return "", "", "", errUnsupportedShell
   788→	}
   789→}
   790→
   791→// promptShellCompletion offers to add shell completion to the user's rc file.
   792→// Only prompts if completion is not already configured.
   793→func promptShellCompletion(w io.Writer) error {
   794→	shellName, rcFile, completionLine, err := shellCompletionTarget()
   795→	if err != nil {
   796→		if errors.Is(err, errUnsupportedShell) {
   797→			fmt.Fprintf(w, "Note: Shell completion not available for your shell. Supported: zsh, bash, fish.\n")
   798→			return nil
   799→		}
   800→		return fmt.Errorf("shell completion: %w", err)
   801→	}
   802→
   803→	if isCompletionConfigured(rcFile) {
   804→		fmt.Fprintf(w, "✓ Shell completion already configured in %s\n", rcFile)
   805→		return nil
   806→	}
   807→
   808→	var selected string
   809→	form := NewAccessibleForm(
   810→		huh.NewGroup(
   811→			huh.NewSelect[string]().
   812→				Title(fmt.Sprintf("Enable shell completion? (detected: %s)", shellName)).
   813→				Options(
   814→					huh.NewOption("Yes", "yes"),
   815→					huh.NewOption("No", "no"),
   816→				).
   817→				Value(&selected),
   818→		),
   819→	)
   820→
   821→	if err := form.Run(); err != nil {
   822→		//nolint:nilerr // User cancelled - not a fatal error, just skip
   823→		return nil
   824→	}
   825→
   826→	if selected != "yes" {
   827→		return nil
   828→	}
   829→
   830→	if err := appendShellCompletion(rcFile, completionLine); err != nil {
   831→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to update %s: %w", rcFile, err)
   832→	}
   833→
   834→	fmt.Fprintf(w, "✓ Shell completion added to %s\n", rcFile)
   835→	fmt.Fprintln(w, "  Restart your shell to activate")
   836→
   837→	return nil
   838→}
   839→
   840→// isCompletionConfigured checks if shell completion is already in the rc file.
   841→func isCompletionConfigured(rcFile string) bool {
   842→	//nolint:gosec // G304: rcFile is constructed from home dir + known filename, not user input
   843→	content, err := os.ReadFile(rcFile)
   844→	if err != nil {
   845→		return false // File doesn't exist or can't read, treat as not configured
   846→	}
   847→	return strings.Contains(string(content), "entire completion")
   848→}
   849→
   850→// appendShellCompletion adds the completion line to the rc file.
   851→func appendShellCompletion(rcFile, completionLine string) error {
   852→	if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(rcFile), 0o700); err != nil {
   853→		return fmt.Errorf("creating directory: %w", err)
   854→	}
   855→	//nolint:gosec // G302: Shell rc files need 0644 for user readability
   856→	f, err := os.OpenFile(rcFile, os.O_APPEND|os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)
   857→	if err != nil {
   858→		return fmt.Errorf("opening file: %w", err)
   859→	}
   860→	defer f.Close()
   861→
   862→	_, err = f.WriteString("\n" + shellCompletionComment + "\n" + completionLine + "\n")
   863→	if err != nil {
   864→		return fmt.Errorf("writing completion: %w", err)
   865→	}
   866→	return nil
   867→}
   868→
   869→// promptTelemetryConsent asks the user if they want to enable telemetry.
   870→// It modifies settings.Telemetry based on the user's choice or flags.
   871→// The caller is responsible for saving settings.
   872→func promptTelemetryConsent(settings *EntireSettings, telemetryFlag bool) error {
   873→	// Handle --telemetry=false flag first (always overrides existing setting)
   874→	if !telemetryFlag {
   875→		f := false
   876→		settings.Telemetry = &f
   877→		return nil
   878→	}
   879→
   880→	// Skip if already asked
   881→	if settings.Telemetry != nil {
   882→		return nil
   883→	}
   884→
   885→	// Skip if env var disables telemetry (record as disabled)
   886→	if os.Getenv("ENTIRE_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT") != "" {
   887→		f := false
   888→		settings.Telemetry = &f
   889→		return nil
   890→	}
   891→
   892→	consent := true // Default to Yes
   893→	form := NewAccessibleForm(
   894→		huh.NewGroup(
   895→			huh.NewConfirm().
   896→				Title("Help improve Entire CLI?").
   897→				Description("Share anonymous usage data. No code or personal info collected.").
   898→				Affirmative("Yes").
   899→				Negative("No").
   900→				Value(&consent),
   901→		),
   902→	)
   903→
   904→	if err := form.Run(); err != nil {
   905→		return fmt.Errorf("telemetry prompt: %w", err)
   906→	}
   907→
   908→	settings.Telemetry = &consent
   909→	return nil
   910→}
   911→
   912→// runUninstall completely removes Entire from the repository.
   913→func runUninstall(w, errW io.Writer, force bool) error {
   914→	// Check if we're in a git repository
   915→	if _, err := paths.RepoRoot(); err != nil {
   916→		fmt.Fprintln(errW, "Not a git repository. Nothing to uninstall.")
   917→		return NewSilentError(errors.New("not a git repository"))
   918→	}
   919→
   920→	// Gather counts for display
   921→	sessionStateCount := countSessionStates()
   922→	shadowBranchCount := countShadowBranches()
   923→	gitHooksInstalled := strategy.IsGitHookInstalled()
   924→	claudeHooksInstalled := checkClaudeCodeHooksInstalled()
   925→	geminiHooksInstalled := checkGeminiCLIHooksInstalled()
   926→	entireDirExists := checkEntireDirExists()
   927→
   928→	// Check if there's anything to uninstall
   929→	if !entireDirExists && !gitHooksInstalled && sessionStateCount == 0 &&
   930→		shadowBranchCount == 0 && !claudeHooksInstalled && !geminiHooksInstalled {
   931→		fmt.Fprintln(w, "Entire is not installed in this repository.")
   932→		return nil
   933→	}
   934→
   935→	// Show confirmation prompt unless --force
   936→	if !force {
   937→		fmt.Fprintln(w, "\nThis will completely remove Entire from this repository:")
   938→		if entireDirExists {
   939→			fmt.Fprintln(w, "  - .entire/ directory")
   940→		}
   941→		if gitHooksInstalled {
   942→			fmt.Fprintln(w, "  - Git hooks (prepare-commit-msg, commit-msg, post-commit, pre-push)")
   943→		}
   944→		if sessionStateCount > 0 {
   945→			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  - Session state files (%d)\n", sessionStateCount)
   946→		}
   947→		if shadowBranchCount > 0 {
   948→			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  - Shadow branches (%d)\n", shadowBranchCount)
   949→		}
   950→		switch {
   951→		case claudeHooksInstalled && geminiHooksInstalled:
   952→			fmt.Fprintln(w, "  - Agent hooks (Claude Code, Gemini CLI)")
   953→		case claudeHooksInstalled:
   954→			fmt.Fprintln(w, "  - Agent hooks (Claude Code)")
   955→		case geminiHooksInstalled:
   956→			fmt.Fprintln(w, "  - Agent hooks (Gemini CLI)")
   957→		}
   958→		fmt.Fprintln(w)
   959→
   960→		var confirmed bool
   961→		form := NewAccessibleForm(
   962→			huh.NewGroup(
   963→				huh.NewConfirm().
   964→					Title("Are you sure you want to uninstall Entire?").
   965→					Affirmative("Yes, uninstall").
   966→					Negative("Cancel").
   967→					Value(&confirmed),
   968→			),
   969→		)
   970→
   971→		if err := form.Run(); err != nil {
   972→			return fmt.Errorf("confirmation cancelled: %w", err)
   973→		}
   974→
   975→		if !confirmed {
   976→			fmt.Fprintln(w, "Uninstall cancelled.")
   977→			return nil
   978→		}
   979→	}
   980→
   981→	fmt.Fprintln(w, "\nUninstalling Entire CLI...")
   982→
   983→	// 1. Remove agent hooks (lowest risk)
   984→	if err := removeAgentHooks(w); err != nil {
   985→		fmt.Fprintf(errW, "Warning: failed to remove agent hooks: %v\n", err)
   986→	}
   987→
   988→	// 2. Remove git hooks
   989→	removed, err := strategy.RemoveGitHook()
   990→	if err != nil {
   991→		fmt.Fprintf(errW, "Warning: failed to remove git hooks: %v\n", err)
   992→	} else if removed > 0 {
   993→		fmt.Fprintf(w, "  Removed git hooks (%d)\n", removed)
   994→	}
   995→
   996→	// 3. Remove session state files
   997→	statesRemoved, err := removeAllSessionStates()
   998→	if err != nil {
   999→		fmt.Fprintf(errW, "Warning: failed to remove session states: %v\n", err)
  1000→	} else if statesRemoved > 0 {
  1001→		fmt.Fprintf(w, "  Removed session states (%d)\n", statesRemoved)
  1002→	}
  1003→
  1004→	// 4. Remove .entire/ directory
  1005→	if err := removeEntireDirectory(); err != nil {
  1006→		fmt.Fprintf(errW, "Warning: failed to remove .entire directory: %v\n", err)
  1007→	} else if entireDirExists {
  1008→		fmt.Fprintln(w, "  Removed .entire directory")
  1009→	}
  1010→
  1011→	// 5. Remove shadow branches
  1012→	branchesRemoved, err := removeAllShadowBranches()
  1013→	if err != nil {
  1014→		fmt.Fprintf(errW, "Warning: failed to remove shadow branches: %v\n", err)
  1015→	} else if branchesRemoved > 0 {
  1016→		fmt.Fprintf(w, "  Removed %d shadow branches\n", branchesRemoved)
  1017→	}
  1018→
  1019→	fmt.Fprintln(w, "\nEntire CLI uninstalled successfully.")
  1020→	return nil
  1021→}
  1022→
  1023→// countSessionStates returns the number of active session state files.
  1024→func countSessionStates() int {
  1025→	store, err := session.NewStateStore()
  1026→	if err != nil {
  1027→		return 0
  1028→	}
  1029→	states, err := store.List(context.Background())
  1030→	if err != nil {
  1031→		return 0
  1032→	}
  1033→	return len(states)
  1034→}
  1035→
  1036→// countShadowBranches returns the number of shadow branches.
  1037→func countShadowBranches() int {
  1038→	branches, err := strategy.ListShadowBranches()
  1039→	if err != nil {
  1040→		return 0
  1041→	}
  1042→	return len(branches)
  1043→}
  1044→
  1045→// checkClaudeCodeHooksInstalled checks if Claude Code hooks are installed.
  1046→func checkClaudeCodeHooksInstalled() bool {
  1047→	ag, err := agent.Get(agent.AgentNameClaudeCode)
  1048→	if err != nil {
  1049→		return false
  1050→	}
  1051→	hookAgent, ok := ag.(agent.HookSupport)
  1052→	if !ok {
  1053→		return false
  1054→	}
  1055→	return hookAgent.AreHooksInstalled()
  1056→}
  1057→
  1058→// checkGeminiCLIHooksInstalled checks if Gemini CLI hooks are installed.
  1059→func checkGeminiCLIHooksInstalled() bool {
  1060→	ag, err := agent.Get(agent.AgentNameGemini)
  1061→	if err != nil {
  1062→		return false
  1063→	}
  1064→	hookAgent, ok := ag.(agent.HookSupport)
  1065→	if !ok {
  1066→		return false
  1067→	}
  1068→	return hookAgent.AreHooksInstalled()
  1069→}
  1070→
  1071→// checkEntireDirExists checks if the .entire directory exists.
  1072→func checkEntireDirExists() bool {
  1073→	entireDirAbs, err := paths.AbsPath(paths.EntireDir)
  1074→	if err != nil {
  1075→		entireDirAbs = paths.EntireDir
  1076→	}
  1077→	_, err = os.Stat(entireDirAbs)
  1078→	return err == nil
  1079→}
  1080→
  1081→// removeAgentHooks removes hooks from all agents that support hooks.
  1082→func removeAgentHooks(w io.Writer) error {
  1083→	var errs []error
  1084→
  1085→	// Remove Claude Code hooks
  1086→	claudeAgent, err := agent.Get(agent.AgentNameClaudeCode)
  1087→	if err == nil {
  1088→		if hookAgent, ok := claudeAgent.(agent.HookSupport); ok {
  1089→			wasInstalled := hookAgent.AreHooksInstalled()
  1090→			if err := hookAgent.UninstallHooks(); err != nil {
  1091→				errs = append(errs, err)
  1092→			} else if wasInstalled {
  1093→				fmt.Fprintln(w, "  Removed Claude Code hooks")
  1094→			}
  1095→		}
  1096→	}
  1097→
  1098→	// Remove Gemini CLI hooks
  1099→	geminiAgent, err := agent.Get(agent.AgentNameGemini)
  1100→	if err == nil {
  1101→		if hookAgent, ok := geminiAgent.(agent.HookSupport); ok {
  1102→			wasInstalled := hookAgent.AreHooksInstalled()
  1103→			if err := hookAgent.UninstallHooks(); err != nil {
  1104→				errs = append(errs, err)
  1105→			} else if wasInstalled {
  1106→				fmt.Fprintln(w, "  Removed Gemini CLI hooks")
  1107→			}
  1108→		}
  1109→	}
  1110→
  1111→	return errors.Join(errs...)
  1112→}
  1113→
  1114→// removeAllSessionStates removes all session state files and the directory.
  1115→func removeAllSessionStates() (int, error) {
  1116→	store, err := session.NewStateStore()
  1117→	if err != nil {
  1118→		return 0, fmt.Errorf("failed to create state store: %w", err)
  1119→	}
  1120→
  1121→	// Count states before removing
  1122→	states, err := store.List(context.Background())
  1123→	if err != nil {
  1124→		return 0, fmt.Errorf("failed to list session states: %w", err)
  1125→	}
  1126→	count := len(states)
  1127→
  1128→	// Remove the entire directory
  1129→	if err := store.RemoveAll(); err != nil {
  1130→		return 0, fmt.Errorf("failed to remove session states: %w", err)
  1131→	}
  1132→
  1133→	return count, nil
  1134→}
  1135→
  1136→// removeEntireDirectory removes the .entire directory.
  1137→func removeEntireDirectory() error {
  1138→	entireDirAbs, err := paths.AbsPath(paths.EntireDir)
  1139→	if err != nil {
  1140→		entireDirAbs = paths.EntireDir
  1141→	}
  1142→	if err := os.RemoveAll(entireDirAbs); err != nil {
  1143→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to remove .entire directory: %w", err)
  1144→	}
  1145→	return nil
  1146→}
  1147→
  1148→// removeAllShadowBranches removes all shadow branches.
  1149→func removeAllShadowBranches() (int, error) {
  1150→	branches, err := strategy.ListShadowBranches()
  1151→	if err != nil {
  1152→		return 0, fmt.Errorf("failed to list shadow branches: %w", err)
  1153→	}
  1154→	if len(branches) == 0 {
  1155→		return 0, nil
  1156→	}
  1157→	deleted, _, err := strategy.DeleteShadowBranches(branches)
  1158→	return len(deleted), err
  1159→}
  1160→

content.text:
Now I have a complete picture of the agent system. Here is a thorough report of all the files and patterns you need to implement a new Cursor agent.

---

## Complete Agent System Architecture

### 1. Agent Interface and Core Types (`cmd/entire/cli/agent/`)

#### `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent/agent.go` -- Core Agent Interface

The `Agent` interface has **18 methods** organized into four groups:

**Identity (5 methods):**
- `Name() AgentName` -- registry key (e.g., `"claude-code"`, `"gemini"`)
- `Type() AgentType` -- display name stored in metadata (e.g., `"Claude Code"`, `"Gemini CLI"`)
- `Description() string` -- human-readable description for UI
- `DetectPresence() (bool, error)` -- checks if agent is configured in the repo (looks for `.claude/` or `.gemini/` dir)
- `ProtectedDirs() []string` -- directories never modified during rewind (e.g., `[".claude"]`)

**Event Mapping (2 methods):**
- `HookNames() []string` -- hook verb subcommands under `entire hooks <agent>`
- `ParseHookEvent(hookName string, stdin io.Reader) (*Event, error)` -- translates native hook into normalized lifecycle Event. Returns `nil` for hooks with no lifecycle significance.

**Transcript Storage (3 methods):**
- `ReadTranscript(sessionRef string) ([]byte, error)` -- reads raw transcript bytes
- `ChunkTranscript(content []byte, maxSize int) ([][]byte, error)` -- format-aware chunking (JSONL splits at line boundaries, JSON splits message arrays)
- `ReassembleTranscript(chunks [][]byte) ([]byte, error)` -- reverses chunking

**Legacy (8 methods) -- will move to optional interfaces later:**
- `GetHookConfigPath() string`
- `SupportsHooks() bool`
- `ParseHookInput(hookType HookType, reader io.Reader) (*HookInput, error)`
- `GetSessionID(input *HookInput) string`
- `GetSessionDir(repoPath string) (string, error)`
- `ResolveSessionFile(sessionDir, agentSessionID string) string`
- `ReadSession(input *HookInput) (*AgentSession, error)`
- `WriteSession(session *AgentSession) error`
- `FormatResumeCommand(sessionID string) string`

**Optional interfaces** (implement as needed):
- `HookSupport` -- `InstallHooks`, `UninstallHooks`, `AreHooksInstalled`, `GetSupportedHooks`
- `HookHandler` -- `GetHookNames()` (backward compat, delegates from `HookNames()`)
- `FileWatcher` -- for agents without hooks (file-based detection)
- `TranscriptAnalyzer` -- `GetTranscriptPosition`, `ExtractModifiedFilesFromOffset`, `ExtractPrompts`, `ExtractSummary`
- `TranscriptPreparer` -- `PrepareTranscript` (e.g., Claude waits for async flush)
- `TokenCalculator` -- `CalculateTokenUsage`
- `SubagentAwareExtractor` -- `ExtractAllModifiedFiles`, `CalculateTotalTokenUsage` (for agents with subagents)

#### `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent/types.go` -- HookType, HookInput, TokenUsage

Key types:
- `HookType` string constants: `HookSessionStart`, `HookSessionEnd`, `HookUserPromptSubmit`, `HookStop`, `HookPreToolUse`, `HookPostToolUse`
- `HookInput` struct with normalized fields: `HookType`, `SessionID`, `SessionRef`, `Timestamp`, `UserPrompt`, `ToolName`, `ToolUseID`, `ToolInput`, `ToolResponse`, `RawData`
- `TokenUsage` struct: `InputTokens`, `CacheCreationTokens`, `CacheReadTokens`, `OutputTokens`, `APICallCount`, `SubagentTokens`
- `SessionChange` struct for `FileWatcher`

#### `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent/event.go` -- Lifecycle Events

`EventType` constants:
- `SessionStart`, `TurnStart`, `TurnEnd`, `Compaction`, `SessionEnd`, `SubagentStart`, `SubagentEnd`

`Event` struct fields: `Type`, `SessionID`, `PreviousSessionID`, `SessionRef`, `Prompt`, `Timestamp`, `ToolUseID`, `SubagentID`, `ToolInput`, `ResponseMessage`, `Metadata`

#### `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent/registry.go` -- Agent Registration

Key pattern: **Factory-based self-registration via `init()`**

```go
var registry = make(map[AgentName]Factory)
type Factory func() Agent

func Register(name AgentName, factory Factory)
func Get(name AgentName) (Agent, error)
func List() []AgentName  // sorted
func Detect() (Agent, error)  // checks DetectPresence() on each
func GetByAgentType(agentType AgentType) (Agent, error)
func AllProtectedDirs() []string  // union from all agents
func Default() Agent  // returns DefaultAgentName (claude-code)
```

Agent name/type constants:
```go
AgentNameClaudeCode AgentName = "claude-code"
AgentNameGemini     AgentName = "gemini"

AgentTypeClaudeCode AgentType = "Claude Code"
AgentTypeGemini     AgentType = "Gemini CLI"
AgentTypeUnknown    AgentType = "Agent"

DefaultAgentName = AgentNameClaudeCode
```

For a Cursor agent, you would add:
```go
AgentNameCursor AgentName = "cursor"
AgentTypeCursor AgentType = "Cursor"
```

#### `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent/chunking.go` -- Transcript Chunking

- `MaxChunkSize = 50MB` (GitHub blob limit safety margin)
- `ChunkTranscript(content, agentType)` -- dispatches to agent-specific chunking
- `ReassembleTranscript(chunks, agentType)` -- dispatches to agent-specific reassembly
- `ChunkJSONL(content, maxSize)` -- splits JSONL at newline boundaries (default)
- `ReassembleJSONL(chunks)` -- concatenates with newlines
- `ChunkFileName(baseName, index)` / `ParseChunkIndex(filename, baseName)` / `SortChunkFiles` -- chunk file naming
- `DetectAgentTypeFromContent(content)` -- checks if content is Gemini JSON format

#### `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent/session.go` -- AgentSession

`AgentSession` struct: `SessionID`, `AgentName`, `RepoPath`, `SessionRef`, `StartTime`, `NativeData` (raw bytes in agent's native format), `ModifiedFiles`, `NewFiles`, `DeletedFiles`, `Entries`

Sessions are **not interoperable** between agents. Each agent reads/writes its own format.

---

### 2. Claude Code Agent (`cmd/entire/cli/agent/claudecode/`)

This is the reference implementation. Files:

#### `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent/claudecode/claude.go` -- Main Implementation

- Self-registers in `init()`: `agent.Register(agent.AgentNameClaudeCode, NewClaudeCodeAgent)`
- Struct: `ClaudeCodeAgent struct{}` (empty, stateless)
- `DetectPresence()` checks for `.claude/` directory at repo root
- `GetSessionDir()` returns `~/.claude/projects/<sanitized-path>/`
- `ResolveSessionFile()` returns `<dir>/<id>.jsonl`
- Transcript format: **JSONL** (one JSON object per line)
- `GetTranscriptPosition()` counts lines (lightweight, no JSON parse)
- `ExtractModifiedFilesFromOffset()` parses JSONL from offset, extracts files from Write/Edit tools
- `ChunkTranscript()` / `ReassembleTranscript()` delegate to `agent.ChunkJSONL` / `agent.ReassembleJSONL`
- `ExtractAllModifiedFiles()` and `CalculateTotalTokenUsage()` include subagent transcripts
- `FormatResumeCommand()` returns `"claude -r " + sessionID`

#### `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent/claudecode/types.go` -- Claude-Specific Types

- `ClaudeSettings`, `ClaudeHooks`, `ClaudeHookMatcher`, `ClaudeHookEntry` -- settings.json structure
- `sessionInfoRaw`, `userPromptSubmitRaw`, `taskHookInputRaw`, `postToolHookInputRaw` -- raw JSON from hooks
- File modification tool names: `Write`, `Edit`, `NotebookEdit`, `mcp__acp__Write`, `mcp__acp__Edit`
- `messageUsage` / `messageWithUsage` -- for token calculation

#### `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent/claudecode/hooks.go` -- Hook Installation

- Implements `HookSupport` and `HookHandler` interfaces
- `GetHookNames()` returns 7 hooks: `session-start`, `session-end`, `stop`, `user-prompt-submit`, `pre-task`, `post-task`, `post-todo`
- `InstallHooks()` reads/modifies `.claude/settings.json` preserving unknown fields
- Also adds `permissions.deny` rule to block Claude from reading metadata
- Commands use form: `"entire hooks claude-code <verb>"` (or `"go run ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/..."` for localDev)
- `entireHookPrefixes` used to identify Entire hooks
- `UninstallHooks()` removes only Entire hooks, preserving user hooks
- `AreHooksInstalled()` checks for known command patterns

#### `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent/claudecode/transcript.go` -- Transcript Parsing

- `TranscriptLine` is an alias for `transcript.Line`
- `ExtractModifiedFiles()` -- scans assistant messages for tool_use blocks with file modification tools
- `ExtractLastUserPrompt()` -- handles both string and array content formats
- `TruncateAtUUID()` -- returns lines up to UUID
- `FindCheckpointUUID()` -- finds tool_result for a given tool_use_id
- `CalculateTokenUsage()` -- deduplicates by message.id (handles streaming duplicates)
- `ExtractSpawnedAgentIDs()` -- finds "agentId: <id>" in Task tool results
- `CalculateTotalTokenUsage()` / `ExtractAllModifiedFiles()` -- include subagent data

#### `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent/claudecode/lifecycle.go` -- Lifecycle Event Parsing

- Compile-time assertions: implements `TranscriptAnalyzer`, `TranscriptPreparer`, `TokenCalculator`, `SubagentAwareExtractor`
- `HookNames()` delegates to `GetHookNames()`
- `ParseHookEvent()` -- the key switch statement mapping hook names to event types:
  - `session-start` -> `SessionStart`
  - `user-prompt-submit` -> `TurnStart`
  - `stop` -> `TurnEnd`
  - `session-end` -> `SessionEnd`
  - `pre-task` -> `SubagentStart`
  - `post-task` -> `SubagentEnd`
  - `post-todo` -> `nil` (handled separately)
- `readAndParse[T]()` -- generic helper to read stdin and unmarshal JSON
- `PrepareTranscript()` -- waits for Claude's async transcript flush (polls for sentinel)
- `waitForTranscriptFlush()` -- polls transcript tail for `stopHookSentinel` within 3s
- `ExtractPrompts()`, `ExtractSummary()` -- delegate to transcript parsing

---

### 3. Gemini CLI Agent (`cmd/entire/cli/agent/geminicli/`)

This is the second implementation, useful as a reference for a different transcript format.

#### `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent/geminicli/gemini.go` -- Main Implementation

- `DetectPresence()` checks for `.gemini/` directory
- `GetSessionDir()` returns `~/.gemini/tmp/<sanitized-path>/chats/`
- `ResolveSessionFile()` uses pattern `session-*-<first8chars>.json` (Gemini's naming convention)
- Transcript format: **JSON** with `{"messages": [...]}` structure (NOT JSONL)
- `GetTranscriptPosition()` returns message count (not line count)
- `ExtractModifiedFilesFromOffset()` iterates messages from offset
- File modification tools: `write_file`, `edit_file`, `save_file`, `replace`
- `ChunkTranscript()` splits messages array preserving JSON structure
- `ReassembleTranscript()` merges message arrays from chunks
- `FormatResumeCommand()` returns `"gemini --resume " + sessionID`

#### `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent/geminicli/types.go` -- Gemini-Specific Types

- `GeminiSettings`, `GeminiHooksConfig`, `GeminiHooks`, `GeminiHookMatcher`, `GeminiHookEntry`
- Notable: `GeminiHookEntry` has a `Name` field (required, unlike Claude)
- Hook type names use different convention: `SessionStart`, `BeforeAgent`, `AfterAgent`, `BeforeModel`, `AfterModel`, `BeforeToolSelection`, `BeforeTool`, `AfterTool`, `PreCompress`, `Notification`
- `sessionInfoRaw` has extra fields: `Cwd`, `HookEventName`, `Source`, `Reason`
- `geminiMessageTokens` with `Input`, `Output`, `Cached`, `Thoughts`, `Tool`, `Total`

#### `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent/geminicli/hooks.go` -- Hook Installation

- 11 hook names (more than Claude's 7): `session-start`, `session-end`, `before-agent`, `after-agent`, `before-model`, `after-model`, `before-tool-selection`, `before-tool`, `after-tool`, `pre-compress`, `notification`
- `InstallHooks()` sets `hooksConfig.enabled = true` (Gemini requires this)
- Installs 12 hooks total (session-end installs two: exit + logout matchers)
- Uses `entireHookPrefixes` with `"entire "` and `"go run ${GEMINI_PROJECT_DIR}/..."`
- Has idempotency check (returns 0 if same hooks already installed)

#### `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent/geminicli/lifecycle.go` -- Lifecycle Event Parsing

- Implements `TranscriptAnalyzer`, `TokenCalculator`
- Does NOT implement `TranscriptPreparer` or `SubagentAwareExtractor`
- `ParseHookEvent()` mappings:
  - `session-start` -> `SessionStart`
  - `before-agent` -> `TurnStart` (Gemini's equivalent of UserPromptSubmit)
  - `after-agent` -> `TurnEnd` (Gemini's equivalent of Stop)
  - `session-end` -> `SessionEnd`
  - `pre-compress` -> `Compaction` (unique to Gemini)
  - `before-tool`, `after-tool`, `before-model`, `after-model`, `before-tool-selection`, `notification` -> `nil` (no lifecycle action)
- `readAndParse[T]()` -- same generic pattern as Claude

#### `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent/geminicli/transcript.go` -- Transcript Parsing

- `GeminiTranscript` struct with `Messages []GeminiMessage`
- `GeminiMessage` has custom `UnmarshalJSON` to handle both string and array content
- `GeminiToolCall` with `Args map[string]interface{}`
- File path extraction checks `file_path`, `path`, `filename` args

- `CalculateTokenUsage()` / `CalculateTokenUsageFromFile()` -- processes `tokens` object per message

The "(Preview)" label for Gemini appears in `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/setup.go` at line 620-629:
```go
if agentName == agent.AgentNameGemini {
    msg += " (Preview)"
}
```

---

### 4. Hook System

#### `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/hooks_cmd.go` -- Hook Command Tree

- Creates `entire hooks` command (hidden, internal)
- Adds `entire hooks git` subcommand for strategy-level git hooks
- **Dynamically adds agent subcommands**: iterates `agent.List()`, casts to `HookHandler`, calls `newAgentHooksCmd()`
- Imports agents to ensure registration: `_ "github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent/claudecode"` and `_ "github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent/geminicli"`

**For a new Cursor agent, you would add:**
```go
_ "github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent/cursor"
```

#### `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/hook_registry.go` -- Hook Dispatch

- `currentHookAgentName` -- tracks which agent is running the current hook
- `GetCurrentHookAgent()` -- returns agent from hook context
- `newAgentHooksCmd()` -- creates `entire hooks <agentName>` command with logging setup
- `newAgentHookVerbCmdWithLogging()` -- creates individual verb commands (e.g., `entire hooks claude-code stop`)
- **Critical dispatch logic:**
  1. Checks if in git repo and Entire is enabled (silent skip if not)
  2. Gets agent via `agent.Get(agentName)`
  3. Calls `ag.ParseHookEvent(hookName, cmd.InOrStdin())` to get normalized Event
  4. If event is non-nil: calls `DispatchLifecycleEvent(ag, event)` (generic dispatcher)
  5. Special case: Claude's `post-todo` is handled directly via `handleClaudeCodePostTodo()`
  6. All other nil events are no-ops

#### `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/lifecycle.go` -- Generic Lifecycle Dispatcher

`DispatchLifecycleEvent(ag, event)` routes events to handlers:
- `SessionStart` -> shows banner, checks concurrent sessions, fires state machine
- `TurnStart` -> captures pre-prompt state (including transcript position), ensures strategy setup, initializes session
- `TurnEnd` -> validates transcript, copies it to metadata dir, extracts prompts/summary/modified files, detects git status changes, calculates tokens, calls `strat.SaveStep()`, transitions phase
- `Compaction` -> resets transcript offset, stays in ACTIVE phase
- `SessionEnd` -> marks session as ended
- `SubagentStart` -> captures pre-task state
- `SubagentEnd` -> detects changes, saves task checkpoint via `strat.SaveTaskStep()`

---

### 5. Transcript Package (`cmd/entire/cli/transcript/`)

#### `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/transcript/types.go` -- Shared Types

Claude-specific JSONL types used by both the transcript package and claudecode package:
```go
type Line struct {
    Type    string          `json:"type"`    // "user" or "assistant"
    UUID    string          `json:"uuid"`
    Message json.RawMessage `json:"message"`
}
type UserMessage struct { Content interface{} }
type AssistantMessage struct { Content []ContentBlock }
type ContentBlock struct { Type, Text, Name string; Input json.RawMessage }
type ToolInput struct { FilePath, NotebookPath, Description, Command, Pattern, Skill, URL, Prompt string }
```

Constants: `TypeUser = "user"`, `TypeAssistant = "assistant"`, `ContentTypeText = "text"`, `ContentTypeToolUse = "tool_use"`

#### `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/transcript/parse.go` -- Parsing Logic

- `ParseFromBytes(content)` -- parses JSONL byte slice into `[]Line`
- `ParseFromFileAtLine(path, startLine)` -- reads file starting from line offset, returns `([]Line, totalLines, error)`
- `SliceFromLine(content, startLine)` -- returns byte content starting from line offset
- `ExtractUserContent(message)` -- extracts text from user messages (handles string and array formats), strips IDE context tags

---

### 6. Setup Flow (`cmd/entire/cli/setup.go`)

#### `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/setup.go` -- Enable/Disable Commands

**`entire enable` command** supports:
- `--agent <name>` -- non-interactive mode for specific agent
- `--strategy <name>` -- manual-commit or auto-commit
- `--local-dev` -- use `go run` instead of binary
- `--force` / `-f` -- force reinstall hooks
- `--skip-push-sessions` -- disable session log pushing
- `--telemetry` -- enable/disable analytics

**Enable flow:**
1. Check git repository
2. Detect/select agent
3. Install agent hooks (`ag.(HookSupport).InstallHooks()`)
4. Create `.entire/` directory with `.gitignore`
5. Save settings (strategy, enabled flag, etc.)
6. Install git hooks (`strategy.InstallGitHook()`)
7. Prompt telemetry consent (interactive mode)
8. Call `strat.EnsureSetup()`

**`setupAgentHooksNonInteractive()`** is the key function for `--agent` mode:
- Casts to `HookSupport`, installs hooks
- Appends "(Preview)" suffix for Gemini agent
- Saves settings, installs git hooks, calls strategy setup

**`isFullyEnabled()`** checks: settings exist + enabled, any agent hooks installed, git hooks installed, `.entire/` dir exists.

**`entire disable --uninstall`** removes: agent hooks (all registered), git hooks, session states, `.entire/` directory, shadow branches.

---

### Key Patterns for Implementing a Cursor Agent

1. **Create package** `cmd/entire/cli/agent/cursor/` with files following the same structure:
   - `cursor.go` -- main implementation with `init()` self-registration
   - `types.go` -- Cursor-specific types (settings structure, hook input formats, file modification tool names)
   - `hooks.go` -- `HookSupport`/`HookHandler` implementation (install/uninstall/check hooks)
   - `transcript.go` -- Cursor transcript parsing (format depends on what Cursor uses)
   - `lifecycle.go` -- `ParseHookEvent()` mapping Cursor hooks to lifecycle events, optional interface implementations

2. **Register constants** in `registry.go`:
   ```go
   AgentNameCursor AgentName = "cursor"
   AgentTypeCursor AgentType = "Cursor"
   ```

3. **Import for side-effect registration** in `hooks_cmd.go`:
   ```go
   _ "github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent/cursor"
   ```

4. **Implement the Agent interface** -- all 18 methods plus `HookSupport` and `HookHandler`

5. **Map Cursor's hook names** to lifecycle events in `ParseHookEvent()`. The minimum required events are: `SessionStart`, `TurnStart`, `TurnEnd`, `SessionEnd`

6. **Handle Cursor's transcript format** -- implement `ReadTranscript`, `ChunkTranscript`, `ReassembleTranscript` and optionally `TranscriptAnalyzer` if you want rich metadata extraction

7. **Update setup.go** -- add Cursor to `removeAgentHooks()`, `checkCursorHooksInstalled()`, and potentially add `(Preview)` label like Gemini

8. **Add compile-time interface assertions** like both existing agents do:
   ```go
   var _ agent.HookSupport = (*CursorAgent)(nil)
   ```

content.text:
Now I have a complete picture of the agent system. Here is a thorough report of all the files and patterns you need to implement a new Cursor agent.

---

## Complete Agent System Architecture

### 1. Agent Interface and Core Types (`cmd/entire/cli/agent/`)

#### `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent/agent.go` -- Core Agent Interface

The `Agent` interface has **18 methods** organized into four groups:

**Identity (5 methods):**
- `Name() AgentName` -- registry key (e.g., `"claude-code"`, `"gemini"`)
- `Type() AgentType` -- display name stored in metadata (e.g., `"Claude Code"`, `"Gemini CLI"`)
- `Description() string` -- human-readable description for UI
- `DetectPresence() (bool, error)` -- checks if agent is configured in the repo (looks for `.claude/` or `.gemini/` dir)
- `ProtectedDirs() []string` -- directories never modified during rewind (e.g., `[".claude"]`)

**Event Mapping (2 methods):**
- `HookNames() []string` -- hook verb subcommands under `entire hooks <agent>`
- `ParseHookEvent(hookName string, stdin io.Reader) (*Event, error)` -- translates native hook into normalized lifecycle Event. Returns `nil` for hooks with no lifecycle significance.

**Transcript Storage (3 methods):**
- `ReadTranscript(sessionRef string) ([]byte, error)` -- reads raw transcript bytes
- `ChunkTranscript(content []byte, maxSize int) ([][]byte, error)` -- format-aware chunking (JSONL splits at line boundaries, JSON splits message arrays)
- `ReassembleTranscript(chunks [][]byte) ([]byte, error)` -- reverses chunking

**Legacy (8 methods) -- will move to optional interfaces later:**
- `GetHookConfigPath() string`
- `SupportsHooks() bool`
- `ParseHookInput(hookType HookType, reader io.Reader) (*HookInput, error)`
- `GetSessionID(input *HookInput) string`
- `GetSessionDir(repoPath string) (string, error)`
- `ResolveSessionFile(sessionDir, agentSessionID string) string`
- `ReadSession(input *HookInput) (*AgentSession, error)`
- `WriteSession(session *AgentSession) error`
- `FormatResumeCommand(sessionID string) string`

**Optional interfaces** (implement as needed):
- `HookSupport` -- `InstallHooks`, `UninstallHooks`, `AreHooksInstalled`, `GetSupportedHooks`
- `HookHandler` -- `GetHookNames()` (backward compat, delegates from `HookNames()`)
- `FileWatcher` -- for agents without hooks (file-based detection)
- `TranscriptAnalyzer` -- `GetTranscriptPosition`, `ExtractModifiedFilesFromOffset`, `ExtractPrompts`, `ExtractSummary`
- `TranscriptPreparer` -- `PrepareTranscript` (e.g., Claude waits for async flush)
- `TokenCalculator` -- `CalculateTokenUsage`
- `SubagentAwareExtractor` -- `ExtractAllModifiedFiles`, `CalculateTotalTokenUsage` (for agents with subagents)

#### `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent/types.go` -- HookType, HookInput, TokenUsage

Key types:
- `HookType` string constants: `HookSessionStart`, `HookSessionEnd`, `HookUserPromptSubmit`, `HookStop`, `HookPreToolUse`, `HookPostToolUse`
- `HookInput` struct with normalized fields: `HookType`, `SessionID`, `SessionRef`, `Timestamp`, `UserPrompt`, `ToolName`, `ToolUseID`, `ToolInput`, `ToolResponse`, `RawData`
- `TokenUsage` struct: `InputTokens`, `CacheCreationTokens`, `CacheReadTokens`, `OutputTokens`, `APICallCount`, `SubagentTokens`
- `SessionChange` struct for `FileWatcher`

#### `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent/event.go` -- Lifecycle Events

`EventType` constants:
- `SessionStart`, `TurnStart`, `TurnEnd`, `Compaction`, `SessionEnd`, `SubagentStart`, `SubagentEnd`

`Event` struct fields: `Type`, `SessionID`, `PreviousSessionID`, `SessionRef`, `Prompt`, `Timestamp`, `ToolUseID`, `SubagentID`, `ToolInput`, `ResponseMessage`, `Metadata`

#### `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent/registry.go` -- Agent Registration

Key pattern: **Factory-based self-registration via `init()`**

```go
var registry = make(map[AgentName]Factory)
type Factory func() Agent

func Register(name AgentName, factory Factory)
func Get(name AgentName) (Agent, error)
func List() []AgentName  // sorted
func Detect() (Agent, error)  // checks DetectPresence() on each
func GetByAgentType(agentType AgentType) (Agent, error)
func AllProtectedDirs() []string  // union from all agents
func Default() Agent  // returns DefaultAgentName (claude-code)
```

Agent name/type constants:
```go
AgentNameClaudeCode AgentName = "claude-code"
AgentNameGemini     AgentName = "gemini"

AgentTypeClaudeCode AgentType = "Claude Code"
AgentTypeGemini     AgentType = "Gemini CLI"
AgentTypeUnknown    AgentType = "Agent"

DefaultAgentName = AgentNameClaudeCode
```

For a Cursor agent, you would add:
```go
AgentNameCursor AgentName = "cursor"
AgentTypeCursor AgentType = "Cursor"
```

#### `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent/chunking.go` -- Transcript Chunking

- `MaxChunkSize = 50MB` (GitHub blob limit safety margin)
- `ChunkTranscript(content, agentType)` -- dispatches to agent-specific chunking
- `ReassembleTranscript(chunks, agentType)` -- dispatches to agent-specific reassembly
- `ChunkJSONL(content, maxSize)` -- splits JSONL at newline boundaries (default)
- `ReassembleJSONL(chunks)` -- concatenates with newlines
- `ChunkFileName(baseName, index)` / `ParseChunkIndex(filename, baseName)` / `SortChunkFiles` -- chunk file naming
- `DetectAgentTypeFromContent(content)` -- checks if content is Gemini JSON format

#### `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent/session.go` -- AgentSession

`AgentSession` struct: `SessionID`, `AgentName`, `RepoPath`, `SessionRef`, `StartTime`, `NativeData` (raw bytes in agent's native format), `ModifiedFiles`, `NewFiles`, `DeletedFiles`, `Entries`

Sessions are **not interoperable** between agents. Each agent reads/writes its own format.

---

### 4. Hook System

#### `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/hooks_cmd.go` -- Hook Command Tree

- Creates `entire hooks` command (hidden, internal)
- Adds `entire hooks git` subcommand for strategy-level git hooks
- **Dynamically adds agent subcommands**: iterates `agent.List()`, casts to `HookHandler`, calls `newAgentHooksCmd()`
- Imports agents to ensure registration: `_ "github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent/claudecode"` and `_ "github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent/geminicli"`

**For a new Cursor agent, you would add:**
```go
_ "github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent/cursor"
```

#### `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/hook_registry.go` -- Hook Dispatch

- `currentHookAgentName` -- tracks which agent is running the current hook
- `GetCurrentHookAgent()` -- returns agent from hook context
- `newAgentHooksCmd()` -- creates `entire hooks <agentName>` command with logging setup
- `newAgentHookVerbCmdWithLogging()` -- creates individual verb commands (e.g., `entire hooks claude-code stop`)
- **Critical dispatch logic:**
  1. Checks if in git repo and Entire is enabled (silent skip if not)
  2. Gets agent via `agent.Get(agentName)`
  3. Calls `ag.ParseHookEvent(hookName, cmd.InOrStdin())` to get normalized Event
  4. If event is non-nil: calls `DispatchLifecycleEvent(ag, event)` (generic dispatcher)
  5. Special case: Claude's `post-todo` is handled directly via `handleClaudeCodePostTodo()`
  6. All other nil events are no-ops

#### `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/lifecycle.go` -- Generic Lifecycle Dispatcher

`DispatchLifecycleEvent(ag, event)` routes events to handlers:
- `SessionStart` -> shows banner, checks concurrent sessions, fires state machine
- `TurnStart` -> captures pre-prompt state (including transcript position), ensures strategy setup, initializes session
- `TurnEnd` -> validates transcript, copies it to metadata dir, extracts prompts/summary/modified files, detects git status changes, calculates tokens, calls `strat.SaveStep()`, transitions phase
- `Compaction` -> resets transcript offset, stays in ACTIVE phase
- `SessionEnd` -> marks session as ended
- `SubagentStart` -> captures pre-task state
- `SubagentEnd` -> detects changes, saves task checkpoint via `strat.SaveTaskStep()`

### 6. Setup Flow (`cmd/entire/cli/setup.go`)

#### `/Users/robin/Developer/cli/cmd/entire/cli/setup.go` -- Enable/Disable Commands

**`entire enable` command** supports:
- `--agent <name>` -- non-interactive mode for specific agent
- `--strategy <name>` -- manual-commit or auto-commit
- `--local-dev` -- use `go run` instead of binary
- `--force` / `-f` -- force reinstall hooks
- `--skip-push-sessions` -- disable session log pushing
- `--telemetry` -- enable/disable analytics

**Enable flow:**
1. Check git repository
2. Detect/select agent
3. Install agent hooks (`ag.(HookSupport).InstallHooks()`)
4. Create `.entire/` directory with `.gitignore`
5. Save settings (strategy, enabled flag, etc.)
6. Install git hooks (`strategy.InstallGitHook()`)
7. Prompt telemetry consent (interactive mode)
8. Call `strat.EnsureSetup()`

**`setupAgentHooksNonInteractive()`** is the key function for `--agent` mode:
- Casts to `HookSupport`, installs hooks
- Appends "(Preview)" suffix for Gemini agent
- Saves settings, installs git hooks, calls strategy setup

**`isFullyEnabled()`** checks: settings exist + enabled, any agent hooks installed, git hooks installed, `.entire/` dir exists.

**`entire disable --uninstall`** removes: agent hooks (all registered), git hooks, session states, `.entire/` directory, shadow branches.

### Key Patterns for Implementing a Cursor Agent

1. **Create package** `cmd/entire/cli/agent/cursor/` with files following the same structure:
   - `cursor.go` -- main implementation with `init()` self-registration
   - `types.go` -- Cursor-specific types (settings structure, hook input formats, file modification tool names)
   - `hooks.go` -- `HookSupport`/`HookHandler` implementation (install/uninstall/check hooks)
   - `transcript.go` -- Cursor transcript parsing (format depends on what Cursor uses)
   - `lifecycle.go` -- `ParseHookEvent()` mapping Cursor hooks to lifecycle events, optional interface implementations

2. **Register constants** in `registry.go`:
   ```go
   AgentNameCursor AgentName = "cursor"
   AgentTypeCursor AgentType = "Cursor"
   ```

3. **Import for side-effect registration** in `hooks_cmd.go`:
   ```go
   _ "github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent/cursor"
   ```

4. **Implement the Agent interface** -- all 18 methods plus `HookSupport` and `HookHandler`

5. **Map Cursor's hook names** to lifecycle events in `ParseHookEvent()`. The minimum required events are: `SessionStart`, `TurnStart`, `TurnEnd`, `SessionEnd`

6. **Handle Cursor's transcript format** -- implement `ReadTranscript`, `ChunkTranscript`, `ReassembleTranscript` and optionally `TranscriptAnalyzer` if you want rich metadata extraction

7. **Update setup.go** -- add Cursor to `removeAgentHooks()`, `checkCursorHooksInstalled()`, and potentially add `(Preview)` label like Gemini

8. **Add compile-time interface assertions** like both existing agents do:
   ```go
   var _ agent.HookSupport = (*CursorAgent)(nil)
   ```

     9→var (

    11→	registry   = make(map[AgentName]Factory)
    12→)
    13→
    14→// Factory creates a new agent instance
    15→type Factory func() Agent
    16→
    17→// Register adds an agent factory to the registry.
    18→// Called from init() in each agent implementation.
    19→func Register(name AgentName, factory Factory) {
    20→	registryMu.Lock()
    21→	defer registryMu.Unlock()
    22→	registry[name] = factory
    23→}
    24→
    25→// Get retrieves an agent by name.
    26→//
    27→
    28→func Get(name AgentName) (Agent, error) {
    29→	registryMu.RLock()
    30→	defer registryMu.RUnlock()
    31→
    32→	factory, ok := registry[name]
    33→	if !ok {
    34→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown agent: %s (available: %v)", name, List())
    35→	}
    36→	return factory(), nil
    37→}
    38→
    39→// List returns all registered agent names in sorted order.
    40→func List() []AgentName {
    41→	registryMu.RLock()
    42→	defer registryMu.RUnlock()
    43→
    44→	names := make([]AgentName, 0, len(registry))
    45→	for name := range registry {
    46→		names = append(names, name)
    47→	}
    48→	slices.Sort(names)
    49→	return names
    50→}
    51→
    52→// Detect attempts to auto-detect which agent is being used.
    53→// Checks each registered agent's DetectPresence method.
    54→//
    55→
    56→func Detect() (Agent, error) {
    57→	registryMu.RLock()
    58→	defer registryMu.RUnlock()
    59→
    60→	for _, factory := range registry {
    61→		ag := factory()
    62→		if present, err := ag.DetectPresence(); err == nil && present {
    63→			return ag, nil
    64→		}
    65→	}
    66→	return nil, fmt.Errorf("no agent detected (available: %v)", List())
    67→}
    68→
    69→// AgentName is the registry key type for agents (e.g., "claude-code", "gemini").
    70→//
    71→//nolint:revive // stuttering is intentional - distinguishes from AgentType when both are used
    72→type AgentName string
    73→
    74→// AgentType is the display name type stored in metadata/trailers (e.g., "Claude Code", "Gemini CLI").
    75→//
    76→//nolint:revive // stuttering is intentional - distinguishes from AgentName when both are used
    77→type AgentType string
    78→
    79→// Agent name constants (registry keys)
    80→const (
    81→	AgentNameClaudeCode AgentName = "claude-code"
    82→	AgentNameGemini     AgentName = "gemini"
    83→)
    84→

    88→	AgentTypeGemini     AgentType = "Gemini CLI"
    89→	AgentTypeUnknown    AgentType = "Agent" // Fallback for backwards compatibility

    95→// GetByAgentType retrieves an agent by its type identifier.
    96→//
    97→// Note: This uses a linear search that instantiates agents until a match is found.
    98→// This is acceptable because:
    99→//   - Agent count is small (~2-20 agents)
   100→//   - Agent factories are lightweight (empty struct allocation)
   101→//   - Called infrequently (commit hooks, rewind, debug commands - not hot paths)
   102→//   - Cost is ~400ns worst case vs milliseconds for I/O operations
   103→//
   104→// Only optimize if agent count exceeds 100 or profiling shows this as a bottleneck.
   105→func GetByAgentType(agentType AgentType) (Agent, error) {
   106→	registryMu.RLock()
   107→	defer registryMu.RUnlock()
   108→
   109→	for _, factory := range registry {
   110→		ag := factory()
   111→		if ag.Type() == agentType {
   112→			return ag, nil
   113→		}
   114→	}
   115→
   116→	return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown agent type: %s", agentType)
   117→}
   118→
   119→// AllProtectedDirs returns the union of ProtectedDirs from all registered agents.
   120→func AllProtectedDirs() []string {
   121→	// Copy factories under the lock, then release before calling external code.
   122→	registryMu.RLock()
   123→	factories := make([]Factory, 0, len(registry))
   124→	for _, f := range registry {
   125→		factories = append(factories, f)
   126→	}
   127→	registryMu.RUnlock()
   128→
   129→	seen := make(map[string]struct{})
   130→	var dirs []string
   131→	for _, factory := range factories {
   132→		for _, d := range factory().ProtectedDirs() {
   133→			if _, ok := seen[d]; !ok {
   134→				seen[d] = struct{}{}
   135→				dirs = append(dirs, d)
   136→			}
   137→		}
   138→	}
   139→	slices.Sort(dirs)
   140→	return dirs
   141→}

   147→func Default() Agent {
   148→	a, _ := Get(DefaultAgentName)
   149→	return a
   150→}
